title: rootlessport exited unexpectedly while container still running
body:
### Issue Description

I run a container in rootless mode, expose port 18066 with `-p`.
It works as expected for about 3 or 4 days before I can't connect to port 18066.
`podman ps` shows container is still running, 
`ss -anop | grep 18066`  --- no result, no process is listening 18066
`ps axuf | grep rootlessport` can't find `rootlessport` process. 

**Seems `rootlessport` exited while container still running**
 I can't found any logs about rootlessport, and have no idea about this weird result.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -d -p 18066:18066 --network podman_dual  -- cb2e104b1f18
2. let container keep running for a few days


### Describe the results you received

`rootlessport` exited while container still running.

### Describe the results you expected

`rootlessport` should keep running while container is running with a `-p` parameter

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Something is killing the process or the process itself is crashing or something. Do you only run the command from the cli? Systemd will kill processes on logout but that should include the contianer (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#17-rootless-containers-exit-once-the-user-session-exits)
Is the slirp4netns process for the container still running?

Can you strace the process until it fails the next time?

> Something is killing the process or the process itself is crashing or something. Do you only run the command from the cli? Systemd will kill processes on logout but that should include the contianer (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#17-rootless-containers-exit-once-the-user-session-exits) Is the slirp4netns process for the container still running?
> 
> Can you strace the process until it fails the next time?
- Yes, slirp4netns is stilling running. and enable-linger is done.

- I run `strace -t -i -f -p xxx -o podman.log` in tmux , till now, it has been five days, `rootlessport` is still running, maybe I should run strace in backgroud ?

I can't reproduce this after update my OS, container keep running for about two weeks, and rootlessport still running.
close this issue
